title: HITL approval not surfaced in dashboard for CLI-launched optimizer

comments.author: ryan
comments.text:
plexus score optimize uses TerminalHITLAdapter (scores.py ~1561-1566), which prompts Approve? [y/n]: at the terminal. The dashboard never shows a HITL panel for CLI-launched runs.

Desired behavior: When the optimizer reaches the approval gate, a HITL pending panel should appear in the dashboard so the user can approve from there.

Implementation path: Switch scores.py to use PlexusHITLAdapter (hitl.py) instead of TerminalHITLAdapter. This will cause the procedure to pause and write a PENDING_APPROVAL ChatMessage. The user then approves via the dashboard; plexus procedure resume or plexus procedure watch handles the response.

Note: The TerminalHITLAdapter is intentionally injectable (options.pop('hitl_adapter', None) in procedure_executor.py:630) so the change is isolated to the score optimize command.

comments.author: ryan
comments.text: Starting implementation: switch CLI optimizer HITL path from terminal-only prompts to dashboard-compatible PlexusHITLAdapter so approval gates surface as pending dashboard interactions.

comments.author: ryan
comments.text: Implemented single-path HITL for CLI optimizer launchers: removed TerminalHITLAdapter injection from plexus/cli/score/scores.py and plexus/cli/procedure/procedures.py optimize commands. Both now rely on default PlexusHITLAdapter wiring in procedure_executor, so approval gates are surfaced via dashboard/chat instead of terminal prompts. Validation: python -m py_compile plexus/cli/score/scores.py plexus/cli/procedure/procedures.py; pytest -q plexus/cli/procedure/test_procedure_executor_compat.py -k dry_run (1 passed).
